items.prompt: Solve `x + 7 = 19` and show how each step preserves equality.
items.expected_evidence: solve or verify bounded systems of linear equations in multiple representations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates solve or verify bounded systems of linear equations in multiple representations with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Solve `4x = 28`, then check the solution by substitution.
items.expected_evidence: explain why a solution pair must satisfy both equations or inequalities simultaneously
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain why a solution pair must satisfy both equations or inequalities simultaneously with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Match the equation `n - 5 = 12` to a balance or missing-value situation and solve it.
items.expected_evidence: classify bounded system outcomes as one solution, no solution, or infinitely many solutions
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates classify bounded system outcomes as one solution, no solution, or infinitely many solutions with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why subtracting the same value from both sides of an equation preserves the solution set.
items.expected_evidence: solve or verify bounded systems of linear equations in multiple representations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to solve or verify bounded systems of linear equations in multiple representations.

items.prompt: Explain what the variable represents and why inverse operations help isolate it.
items.expected_evidence: explain why a solution pair must satisfy both equations or inequalities simultaneously
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to explain why a solution pair must satisfy both equations or inequalities simultaneously.

items.prompt: A student solves `x + 5 = 13` by writing `x = 13 + 5`. Diagnose the first structural mistake. Focus on constraint intersection misread.
items.expected_evidence: solve or verify bounded systems of linear equations in multiple representations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward solve or verify bounded systems of linear equations in multiple representations.

items.prompt: A student solves `3x = 18` by dividing only the `x`. Explain what went wrong. Focus on simultaneous satisfaction gap.
items.expected_evidence: explain why a solution pair must satisfy both equations or inequalities simultaneously
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward explain why a solution pair must satisfy both equations or inequalities simultaneously.

items.prompt: A gym charges a $12 signup fee plus $5 per class. Write and solve an equation for the number of classes if the total was $32.
items.expected_evidence: solve or verify bounded systems of linear equations in multiple representations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves solve or verify bounded systems of linear equations in multiple representations in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A balance model has three equal bags and 6 loose counters on one side and 24 counters on the other. Write and solve the equation.
items.expected_evidence: explain why a solution pair must satisfy both equations or inequalities simultaneously
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves explain why a solution pair must satisfy both equations or inequalities simultaneously in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Two weeks later, solve `m/3 = 8` and explain how you know the result is reasonable.
items.expected_evidence: solve or verify bounded systems of linear equations in multiple representations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates solve or verify bounded systems of linear equations in multiple representations on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Before solving systems of linear equations and inequalities, complete a prerequisite check on linear equations in two variables and explain how it supports the current equation work.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether linear equations in two variables is secure enough to support systems of linear equations and inequalities
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in linear equations in two variables from a current-node issue in systems of linear equations and inequalities.

items.prompt: Use a quick linear functions multiple representations check to explain whether the learner is blocked by prerequisite structure or by systems of linear equations and inequalities itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether linear functions multiple representations is secure enough to support systems of linear equations and inequalities
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in linear functions multiple representations from a current-node issue in systems of linear equations and inequalities.
